project: Who Owns SpaceX — a plain-English tracker

sources.label: SpaceX's filings on the SEC's EDGAR database
SEC search for funds that list SpaceX in their holdings reports
Expected IPO details, as reported by Reuters, Bloomberg and CNBC (June 2026)
SpaceX's IPO filing — the ownership section

notes: Right now, before the IPO, who gets shares is private — it isn't announced as it happens. The “Funds that own it” tab lists mutual funds that already report owning a piece of SpaceX in their official holdings reports.

Most of SpaceX is owned privately — by insiders, employees, and venture-capital firms — and never shows up in fund reports. The “Insiders & big owners” tab comes from SpaceX's IPO filing, which lists everyone who owns more than 5% of a class of shares, plus the company's officers and directors.
Many funds listed here are common 401(k) and IRA choices (their ticker codes are shown), and target-date funds can own SpaceX indirectly — see the “Retirement & 401(k)” tab.
The “Big investors” tab fills in after those investors file their first quarterly holdings reports covering the IPO quarter, around mid-August 2026.

Fund totals add up every share class from each fund's most recent report. The “Value at IPO price” figures under “Insiders & big owners” are just the share count times the expected starting price — not a real market value. Every row links to the official filing it came from.
disclaimer: This site is for information only. It's not financial advice, and it's not an offer to buy or sell anything. The numbers come from public filings, which can lag real life by weeks or months.
